steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content:
The passage opens by noting both groups share the “regional Mexican music” label yet follow “distinct musical traditions.” Sentence 2 describes the former group’s tejano tradition; sentence 3 describes the latter group’s banda tradition. The blank must signal that the second group’s tradition is different from — not a result of or a restatement of — the first group’s.
  • Choice A — ‘therefore,’: Signals logical consequence. The banda tradition does not follow as a result of the tejano tradition. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘to this end,’: Signals that what follows serves the same purpose as what came before. The passage describes the two traditions as distinct and gives them different origins. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘however,’: Signals contrast. After describing the former group’s tejano tradition, “however” correctly marks that the latter follows a different tradition entirely. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘in other words,’: Signals rephrasing or clarification. The banda tradition is not a restatement of the tejano tradition — it is a distinct one. ✗

steps.title: Confirm with the passage’s central claim
steps.content: The passage’s whole point is that despite sharing a label, the two groups follow distinct traditions. “However” reinforces this contrast when introducing the second tradition. That confirms C is correct.
